sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction beyond the public sample PDF is not available yet.
No calibrated visible-trait prediction model is supplied with this fixture.
Some public sample rows are represented as output structure only because this local fixture does not include every underlying trait model.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows trait, result, and description columns across eyes, hair, skin, and weight.
resultRows.description: The sample report table provides the formal row shape that the local agent should preserve.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This anchors report structure and wording fields without treating the sample person as the current user.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived HERC2/OCA2 rs12913832 AG call is available for eye-color context. This does not determine exact eye color.
resultRows.description: The public sample PDF includes blue-vs-brown and blue-vs-green eye-color rows, while MedlinePlus frames eye color as a multi-gene trait.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: OCA2 and HERC2 are important in eye-color genetics, but eye color is polygenic and cannot be reduced to one marker.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MC1R rs1805007 CT call is available for pigmentation context. This is not a diagnosis or a skin-cancer risk result.
resultRows.description: The public sample PDF links MC1R with pheomelanin, red hair, and freckles; local interpretation still depends on supplied evidence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: MC1R is a well-studied pigmentation gene, but many genes and non-genetic factors contribute to hair color and freckles.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived EDAR hair-texture/thickness context row is available. This is context only and does not predict exact hair texture.
resultRows.description: The public sample PDF includes hair curliness and thickness rows, but exact hair texture should not be inferred from one fixture row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair texture and thickness have genetic contributions, but many genes and non-genetic factors can influence the trait.

sampleRows.description: Eye color can range from gray, blue, green, hazel, brown, to dark-brown.

sampleRows.description: Freckles are areas of skin that have an increased amount of a pigment called melanin.

sampleRows.description: The skin's tendency to sunburn rather than tan is a major risk factor for skin cancer.

unavailableTraits: Iris crypts, iris furrows, and iris rings are present in the public sample structure, but no local trait model is supplied for this fixture.
Male pattern baldness and body-mass-index rows are present in the public sample structure, but they are not inferred from this visible-trait fixture.
Skin pigmentation, sun sensitivity, and freckles are limited to educational pigmentation context, not exact appearance or health-risk prediction.
plainEnglishLimits: Visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.
Direct-to-consumer trait tests provide estimates or context, not certainty about exact appearance.
This report must not be used to identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, or report disease risk.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: OCA2/HERC2 and MC1R notes observed in public sample PDF; underlying sample genotypes are not shown

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page sections beyond the public sample PDF have not been extracted.
No calibrated visible-trait probability model is supplied.
Many known and unknown contributors to eye color, hair color, hair texture, skin pigmentation, freckles, baldness, and BMI are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Visible traits can be influenced by multiple genes, development, age, environment, and other non-genetic factors.
The significance of individual visible-trait markers may vary across populations and should not be treated as a deterministic appearance prediction.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, diagnose albinism, or report melanoma risk.
The public sample PDF demonstrates output shape and example rows, not this user's exact final appearance.
